pi_items.source: task.json description line 6: 'Save the completed flipchart to: /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/color_wheel_art.flipchart'
task.json description line 6: explicitly specifies save path
task.json description explicitly requires: Page 1 (Title), Page 2 (Color Wheel), Page 3 (Reference List)
task.json description states 'Primary Colors: Red, Yellow, Blue'; verified against authoritative sources on color theory

task.json description says 'Orange, Green, Violet (or Purple)' indicating Violet OR Purple (not both). Authoritative color wheel sources confirm secondary colors are Orange, Green, and Purple (Purple is the standard term on color wheels, not Violet as a separate secondary color). Verifier.py checks for only 3 secondary colors total.
task.json description lists these elements; verified as the official 7 elements of art against multiple authoritative art education sources
task.json description: 'Create a diagram with at least 6 distinct shapes arranged in a circle or wheel pattern'

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a 3-page ActivInspire flipchart titled 'Color Wheel & Art Elements Lesson' saved to /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/color_wheel_art.flipchart. Page 1 must contain titles 'Color Theory' and 'Elements of Art' with decorative shapes. Page 2 must show a color wheel with at least 6 shapes labeled with the three primary colors (Red, Yellow, Blue) and three secondary colors (Orange, Green, and either Violet or Purple—not both). Page 3 must list all seven elements of art: Line, Shape, Form, Space, Color, Value, and Texture. One metadata value contradicts the task description: the metadata lists both 'Violet' and 'Purple' as separate required secondary colors, but the task description uses '(or Purple)' indicating they are alternatives, and the verifier expects exactly 3 secondary colors total, treating Violet and Purple as one option.
